Identify  

Description

1: Identify

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 50 Skill: Divination
Casting Time: 5 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 100
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Fizzle Adj: 25 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Target Type: Single Spell Type: Beneficial
Source: Live 12/03

Game Description

Provides magical information about the item that your target is holding.

Items with this effect

  • Elbaite Focus
  • Eye of Melnor
  • Fabled Holgresh Grand Vizier Beads
  • Holgresh Grand Vizier Beads
  • Lens of Truth
  • Rod of Identification
  • Silvery Mask
  • Spell: Identify [storebought]
  • Spell: Identify [Magician]
  • Spell: Identify [Wizard]
  • Spell: Identify [Necromancer]
  • Star of Eyes

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Identify
Spell: Identify
Spell: Identify
Spell: Identify
ClassLevel
NEC17
ENC15
MAG13
WIZ14
Expansion:

Duration:
Instant
  • Comments (40)
  • Scroll 1 Sold By (17)
  • Scroll 1 Crafted (1)

Post Comment
Is it still used?
QuoteReply
# Apr 08 2013 at 1:57 PM Rating: Decent
Ridolain
Scholar
280 posts
Can anyone tell me if this spell is still used for ... well what it is supposed to do?

I picked up Salil's Writ pg. 64, and looking it up online, they say there is a left and a right half. So I did what it stated and used Identify on myself (since I need a target) and nothing. I put it in a bag, in a top slot ... no way to tell. Nothing in the description of the writ that says it has been identified.

Is this spell used for anything anymore?
▲ Page top
Is it still used?
QuoteReply
# Apr 08 2013 at 2:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1641773856190
Scholar
2,308 posts
Just to clarify, you have to have the item on your pointer and have yourself targeted for it to work.

QuoteReply
# Jul 31 2006 at 5:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591801614672
61 posts
Then obviously you dont know what it is used for or how it is used
▲ Page top
this spell
QuoteReply
# Nov 18 2006 at 9:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591872415299
14 posts
Be helpful instead of mean!

This spell is used in some different quests to identify different items that are called the same thing, but are actually different. I know that it is used in the Creator missions, and I know of another quest where you need to collect 10 staffs from 10 zones, but they are called the same thing. When you identify them they tell you which zone they are from. I'm sorry for the life of me I cannot remember what these items are called, but the drop from the DSP era of zones. I'm sure there are tons of other quests where you need to identify things as well. Plus, sometimes, items do say amusing things ^_^

For the non-mage there is a quick little quest to get an item called the Star of Eyes. It says mage only, but anyone can use it from inventory. Usefull, especially if you cannot get a mage in your Creators!
▲ Page top
this spell

# May 27 2008 at 7:11 PM Rating: Excellent
Rhykar
View User Forum
Guru
235 posts
items he/she is refering to are Lucid Shards, used for Vex Thal access. (no longer required with the revamp of level restrictions instead of keys to zones)
▲ Page top
this spell
QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2007 at 4:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767064168
Scholar
702 posts
You are absolutely right in its usage. Maybe even a better explaination is quite a few of the Iksar quests involve looting "pages" and with this spell you can Identify which ones you have, therefore you know which ones you are missing and can then shout in the zone for them or head to the Bazaar. Either way this spell can become quite useful on the collection quests.


▲ Page top
this spell

# Feb 09 2004 at 5:02 PM Rating: Decent

If this is the spell I think it is, it's useful for identifying lucid shards for the key to Vex Thal. It can also be used to identify which quarters of the medallion for the PoTactics key.

#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 09 2003 at 1:49 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) THIS SPELL IS PLOP
how do I use this
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2003 at 12:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362287720
Sage
63 posts
I must be missunderstanding this spell because i thought you could target someone cast the spell and find the stats of the persons primary slot or something that's what it sounded like in the discription I'll take another look but will someone please tell me exactly how to use this spell and what it does............would be mist appreciated
▲ Page top
RE: how do I use this
QuoteReply
# Sep 12 2003 at 8:38 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1594570761161
Guru
142 posts
This spell is used to retrieve the lore information from an item. You pick up an item on the cursor and cast identify, it will display any secret text that belongs to it. You can also cast this on anyone else while they have something on the cursor, and they will see the lore info.

An example would be the clay bracelet quest. You have to collect 4 different "runes" from 3 gnome necromancers throughout Norrath and 1 from an aqua goblin in OOT. Each of the runes says Rune of Clay, but when you cast identify it will say left, right, bottom, or top.

I've never found a good use for this spell.

Edited, Fri Sep 12 09:25:15 2003
▲ Page top
RE: how do I use this
QuoteReply
# Dec 19 2005 at 10:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593364048435
10 posts
I've been told it comes in very prominently in instanced zones. It can make the difference between life and death for your party when using the right object can easily beat a certain enemy and using the wrong one will wipe out your party.

I think the main purpose of this spell is for Instances.
▲ Page top
RE: how do I use this
QuoteReply
# Sep 21 2003 at 5:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362287720
Sage
63 posts
thank you for your help

Identifying No-Drop items
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2003 at 11:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769988595
Scholar
21 posts
Was just wondering how a caster can identify an item that is no-drop on another character. Any advise would be helpful.

Edited, Tue Sep 9 12:46:05 2003
▲ Page top
RE: Identifying No-Drop items
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2003 at 12:16 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
The person with the item needs ot hold it on their cursor. You target the character and cast Identify, and THEY will see the item lore.
▲ Page top
Some items have amusing lore...
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2003 at 5:10 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591758665328
36 posts
...if you cast identify, for example, on Shainai's Nightie from Shar Vahl, it says "You beat up a little girl to get this."
▲ Page top
Identify
QuoteReply
# Jul 03 2003 at 9:56 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
To make Identify work, you MUST have the item you want to identify "floating" on your cursor, after that, target yourself and click on the gem that is identify.
▲ Page top
easy
QuoteReply
# Jun 15 2003 at 4:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257874295
64 posts
for the Mage focus items you need "a Torch"
which identifies as "Torch of Alna"

but you can get confused if you buy "a Torch" from a vendor since the names are the same

identify
QuoteReply
# May 21 2003 at 9:14 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591769977715
16 posts
Identifying a fishgill necksash gives you the message "What ye lookin fer fishbreath?"
lolololololol
▲ Page top
What it does
QuoteReply
# Mar 05 2003 at 3:53 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249451805
46 posts
Some items have a bit of text associated with them, and you use Identify to find out what it is.

For example, I had a Black Acrylia Halberd, and when I cast Identify on it, I got the message: "Item lore: shadows swirl on the head of this halberd." Or when I cast Identify on a Shield of Shadows: "Item lore: the shield glares at you."

Sometimes it's just fun stuff like that, or sometimes you need to, for example, collect 10 runes all named the same thing. If you use Identify, you would get a different message for each one.
▲ Page top
What it does
QuoteReply
# Dec 27 2002 at 3:12 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592791513986
24 posts
It Tells you what effect an item has when cast on the item itself.
▲ Page top
